steps.title: Identify the angle
steps.content: From the figure, angle $B$ is a right angle and angle $C$ is $30^\circ$. The angles in a triangle sum to $180^\circ$, so angle $A$ is $60^\circ$.

steps.title: Use the triangle ratio
steps.content: In a $30$-$60$-$90$ triangle, the sides are in the ratio $1:\sqrt{3}:2$. Since $BC=36\sqrt{3}$ is opposite the $60^\circ$ angle, the short leg is $36$ and the hypotenuse is $72$.

steps.title: Find the cosine
steps.content:
For angle $A$, the adjacent leg is $36$ and the hypotenuse is $72$.
$$\cos A=\frac{36}{72}=\frac{1}{2}$$
The answer is 1/2.
